items.q: Which verb is used to say 'I am happy today' (temporary state)?
items.why: Students treat ser and estar as identical. SER for permanent traits, identity, time, origin. ESTAR for temporary states, location, ongoing actions. 'Soy contento' would suggest 'I am a happy person', not 'I'm happy right now'.
Another classic faux ami. Spanish 'constipado' refers to nasal congestion (a cold). The medical 'constipated' is 'estreñido'. Confusion can lead to awkward medical conversations.

items.q: Where do object pronouns go in 'I see him' (lo veo)?
items.why: Students apply English word order. Spanish places object pronouns BEFORE conjugated verbs. They can attach to infinitives, gerunds, and positive commands - but not after a conjugated verb in normal sentences.
Correct answer: Pablo Picasso. “Salvador Dali” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Mexico. “Spain” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Guernica painted?
items.why: Exact answer: 1937. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
